title: Idioms and phrases from Black book 801 1000 Quiz group httpst

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Kangaroo court":
options: A highly respected international tribunal
A false court that doesn't follow legal standards
A court held in a wildlife sanctuary
A court specifically for minor crimes
questions.explanation: A "Kangaroo court" is an unofficial court that disregards the laws and ignores the rights of the accused. (गैर-कानूनी या फर्जी अदालत।)

questions.question: "Look sharp" means:
options: To wear very stylish and pointed shoes
Pay attention
To have a very sharp memory
To look like a needle or knife
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai satark rehna, dhyan dena ya jaldi karna.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In seventh heaven
options: To be at the highest physical altitude possible
Extremely happy or in a state of great delight
To be close to one's death
To be dreaming about a fictional world
questions.explanation: 'In seventh heaven' means to be supremely happy or in a state of extreme bliss. (अत्यधिक खुश या सातवें आसमान पर होना।)

questions.question: What does the idiom "Look to" mean in a professional context?
options: To turn one's eyes away
To ignore a request
To rely on
To search for something lost
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi par bharosa karna ya nirbhar hona (rely on).

questions.question: A person who is "Long in the tooth" is:
options: Having very sharp and white teeth
Very young and energetic
Too old to do something
A very fast eater
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kafi umardaraz ya buddha hona.

questions.question: Identify the meaning of "Hold out an olive branch":
options: To start a new business venture
To plant more trees for the environment
To challenge someone for a fight
To show that you want to end a disagreement with them
questions.explanation: An olive branch is a symbol of peace; thus, this idiom means an offer of reconciliation. (शांति का प्रस्ताव रखना या विवाद खत्म करने की पहल करना।)

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the phrase: "In (the) light of"
options: Considering or taking into account
To be the center of attraction
To ignore the evidence provided
To brighten a dark room with lamps
questions.explanation: In the light of means taking certain facts or information into consideration. (किसी चीज़ को ध्यान में रखते हुए या विचार करते हुए।)

questions.question: What does "Lily-livered" mean?
options: Having a healthy liver
Not brave or cowardly
Someone who loves white flowers
Very kind and generous
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai darpok hona ya jisme sahas ki kami ho.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the long run
options: To be traveling to a very distant location
To participate in a marathon race
To be a very experienced athlete
Eventually, after a long period of time
questions.explanation: 'In the long run' means concerning a longer period of time in the future. (अंततः या लंबे समय के बाद।)

questions.question: What is the meaning of "Let something slip through one’s fingers"?
options: To have a physical injury while working
To wash one's hands off a responsibility
To hold onto a lucky charm tightly
To waste an opportunity to achieve something
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi avsar ya mauke ko hath se nikalne dena ya use ganwa dena (opportunity waste karna).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the flesh
options: To be in a very healthy physical state
To be obsessed with physical appearance
To be related to someone by blood
To be in bodily form or in person
questions.explanation: 'In the flesh' means to see or meet someone in real life, not just in a picture or video. (साक्षात या वास्तविक रूप में उपस्थित होना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the offing
options: Likely to appear or happen soon
To be finishing a very difficult task
To be working in an offshore location
To be going away on a long vacation
questions.explanation: 'In the offing' refers to something that is likely to happen in the near future. (जल्द ही होने वाला या सामने आने वाला।)

questions.question: "Hocus pocus" refers to:
options: To move rapidly from one place to another
A magical healing process
A very serious formal discussion
Meaningless talk or activity, typically designed to trick someone
questions.explanation: Hocus pocus means deceptive talk or nonsense used to fool people. (धोखा देने के लिए की गई अर्थहीन बातें या गतिविधि।)

questions.question: What does "Hold up one's head" mean?
options: To be proud
To be ashamed
To look at the sky
To feel dizzy
questions.explanation: Hold up one's head means to maintain one's dignity or pride. (गर्व महसूस करना या सम्मान के साथ रहना।)

questions.question: What does "Hobson's choice" mean?
options: A choice between many excellent options
A choice made by a group of people
To be in a state of dilemma
No real choice at all / an apparently free choice with no alternative
questions.explanation: Hobson's choice refers to a situation where you must take what is offered or nothing at all. (कोई विकल्प न होना / कहने को विकल्प होना पर वास्तव में एक ही विकल्प होना।)

questions.question: To "Let the grass grow under one’s feet" means:
options: To be a very successful gardener
To wait patiently for the right season
To remain idle
To move very fast towards a goal
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai sust pade rehna, deri karna ya koi kaam na karke khali baithe rehna (time barbad karna).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In black and white
options: To be extremely biased in a situation
In writing or in printed form
To see things only from two extreme perspectives
A situation with no clear moral distinction
questions.explanation: 'In black and white' means something recorded in writing or print. (लिखा हुआ या मुद्रित रूप में होना।)

questions.question: What is the meaning of "In bad taste"?
options: To have a poor sense of food flavors
To be very expensive and luxurious
Not suitable or offensive
To be very polite and kind
questions.explanation: If something is in bad taste, it is considered rude or inappropriate for the occasion. (अनुचित या अपमानजनक होना।)

questions.question: What does "Let down" mean in a personal relationship?
options: To fail
To support someone during a crisis
To make someone feel very proud
To lower a heavy object
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai kisi ko niraash karna ya kisi ki ummeedon par khara na utarna (fail to support).

questions.question: The phrase "Let the chips fall where they may" implies:
options: To be very careful about the final result
Let something happen without bothering about the consequences
To clean up a mess after a big party
To gamble all your money in a casino
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai parinamo (consequences) ki chinta kiye bina kisi cheez ko hone dena—jo hoga dekha jayega.

questions.question: What does the idiom "It takes two to tango" imply?
options: Two people who are exactly similar in nature
Participation of two people is needed for a conflict or action
To solve a problem with the help of a stranger
A dance performance requiring two people
questions.explanation: This idiom means that in a fight or a situation, both parties involved are responsible. (किसी झगड़े या काम के लिए दोनों पक्ष समान रूप से जिम्मेदार होते हैं।)

questions.question: What does the phrase "Hit the ceiling/roof" signify?
options: Explode in anger
To repair a leaking roof
To achieve the highest rank in a company
To jump very high in joy
questions.explanation: Iska arth hota hai achanak bahut zyada gussa ho jana ya aag baboola hona (Explode in anger).

questions.question: If a task is "Like pulling teeth", it is:
options: Done very quickly
Related to medical surgery
Very easy and relaxing
Extremely difficult
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai kisi kaam ka behad mushkil ya kashtdayak hona jise karne mein bahut zor lage.

questions.question: A person who is "Like a dying duck in a thunderstorm" is:
options: Very fast and efficient
Very happy and enjoying the rain
Dejected
Brave and facing the storm
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai bahut udaas, niraash ya hatash dikhna.

questions.question: "In a fog" most nearly means:
options: To be physically lost in the mountains
To see things very clearly
Confused and dazed
To travel in cold weather
questions.explanation: Being in a fog means someone is unable to think clearly or is confused. (भ्रमित और चकराया हुआ होना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the air
options: When an emotion or idea is on everyone's mind or under consideration
To be physically flying or levitating
To be acting in a very arrogant and haughty manner
To be daydreaming and ignoring reality
questions.explanation: 'In the air' refers to a feeling or idea that everyone is aware of or that is currently being discussed. (चर्चा में होना या सबके दिमाग में होना।)

questions.question: Select the meaning of "Hold on":
options: To surrender immediately
To give up a habit
To wait for a short time
To talk loudly
questions.explanation: Hold on is a common phrasal verb meaning to wait briefly. (थोड़े समय के लिए इंतज़ार करना।)

questions.question: If two shops are "Kitty-corner" to each other, they are:
options: Located on the same floor of a mall
Located right next to each other
Located in different cities
Located diagonally opposite each other
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai tirche kone par sthit hona (diagonally opposite).

questions.question: To "Leave no stone unturned" signifies:
options: To look for hidden treasures under rocks
To be a very lazy worker
To give up after the first attempt
Try every possible course of action in order to achieve something
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi lakshya ko paane ke liye har sambhav prayas karna (koi kasar na chhodna).

questions.question: If a person "Let the cat out of the bag", they:
options: Revealed a secret carelessly or by mistake
Rescued a pet from a dangerous situation
Bought a new bag for their kitten
Told a lie to protect a close friend
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai anjane mein ya galti se koi raaz (secret) khol dena ya sabko bata dena.

questions.question: A "Lean and mean" organization is:
options: Large and unorganized
Ready and efficient
Cruel to its employees and customers
Very poor and struggling to survive
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai aisi sanstha jo kaam karne ke liye poori tarah taiyar aur kushal (efficient) ho.

questions.question: If someone "Laughs like a drain", they are:
options: Laughing very loudly
Laughing very quietly and secretly
Laughing at someone's misfortune
Mocking a serious situation
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai bahut zor-zor se hansna (Laugh loudly).

questions.question: What does the idiom "Keep/hold something at bay" imply?
options: To control something and prevent it from causing problems
To store goods near a sea harbor
To encourage someone to approach you
To ignore a problem until it disappears
questions.explanation: To keep something "at bay" means to prevent someone or something from moving closer or causing harm. (किसी समस्या को दूर रखना या उसे बढ़ने से रोकना।)

questions.question: To "Loosen the purse strings" means:
options: To tie a knot very tightly
To increase the money available for expenditure
To become very stingy and greedy
To lose your wallet in a public place
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai kharch ke liye paison ki chhoot dena ya budget badhana.

questions.question: To "Live from hand to mouth" means:
options: To have enough money to live on and nothing extra
To eat food without using any utensils
To be extremely wealthy and greedy
To have a very healthy eating habit
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai bahut mushkil se guzara karna, jitna kamana utna hi kharch ho jana.

questions.question: To "Look down upon" someone means:
options: To help someone who has fallen
To admire someone's success
Regard with contempt or consider someone inferior
To look from the top of a building
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai kisi ko apne se chhota ya tuchh samajhna aur nafrat ki nigah se dekhna.

questions.question: "Hold the fort" means:
options: To take responsibility for a situation temporarily
To attack an enemy's territory
To build a strong building
To escape from a prison
questions.explanation: Hold the fort means to maintain control or take care of affairs while others are away. (अस्थायी रूप से ज़िम्मेदारी संभालना।)

questions.question: What does the idiom "Kick the bucket" signify?
options: To die
To achieve a great milestone
To be very angry and kick objects
To start a cleaning job
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai mrityu ho jana (mar jana).

questions.question: What does the phrase "It's Greek to me" imply?
options: Something that is very easy to solve
A situation related to ancient history
To be proficient in foreign languages
Incomprehensible or impossible to understand
questions.explanation: If something is "Greek to you," it means you cannot understand it at all. (समझ से बाहर होना।)

questions.question: To "Look out" implies:
options: To go outside for a walk
To look through a window
Be careful
To search for something lost
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai savdhan rehna ya hoshiyaar rehna.

questions.question: What does "I don't buy it" mean?
options: I am not convinced
I believe everything you say
I am not interested in shopping
I don't have enough money
questions.explanation: I don't buy it means you do not believe or accept an explanation/excuse. (विश्वास न करना या सहमत न होना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Ins and outs":
options: A person who is very inconsistent
To go in and out of a room repeatedly
The entrance and exit points of a building
Full details or complicated facts of something
questions.explanation: "Ins and outs" refers to the detailed and complex factors or the full story of a situation. (पूर्ण विवरण या जटिल तथ्य।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Keep your chin up":
options: Remain cheerful and hopeful in difficult circumstances
To behave in a very arrogant and proud manner
To look at the sky while walking
To exercise daily to keep the face in shape
questions.explanation: To "keep your chin up" is to stay positive and not get discouraged during tough times. (कठिन परिस्थितियों में भी खुश और आशावादी बने रहना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Keep your wig on":
options: To act like a judge in a formal court
To calm down and stop being angry
To be very careful about one's physical appearance
To wear a hairpiece to cover baldness
questions.explanation: Similar to "keep your shirt on," this idiom is used to tell someone to stop being annoyed or upset. (शांत हो जाना या गुस्सा थूक देना।)

questions.question: What does the idiom "Keep a civil tongue" mean?
options: To speak many languages fluently
To remain silent in a court of law
To argue in a very sophisticated way
To speak with great politeness
questions.explanation: "Keep a civil tongue" means to avoid being rude and to speak politely. (शिष्टता से बात करना।)

questions.question: To "Know something inside out" means:
options: To be confused about the internal parts
To know something thoroughly or everything about it
To have a superficial understanding
To look at only the exterior of an object
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi vishay ki poori aur gehri jankari hona.

questions.question: A person who "Knows how many beans make five" is:
options: Well informed and intelligent
A professional chef or cook
Someone who is obsessed with counting food
Someone who is bad at basic math
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai hoshiyaar aur gyaani hona (jise pata ho ki duniya kaise chalti hai).

questions.question: What does "Ill at ease" mean?
options: To recover quickly from a serious illness
To fall sick due to overworking
To feel uncomfortable or worried in a situation
To be extremely relaxed and confident
questions.explanation: Ill at ease describes a state of anxiety or social awkwardness. (किसी स्थिति में असहज या चिंतित महसूस करना।)

questions.question: To "Kick someone when they are down" implies:
options: To play a game of football aggressively
To cause further trouble to someone already in a difficult situation
To help a fallen person get up
To ignore someone who is asking for help
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi aise vyakti ko aur pareshan karna jo pehle se hi museebat mein ho.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the teeth of
options: To be eating something very hard
In spite of something or directly against opposition
To be suffering from a dental problem
To be talking very rudely to someone
questions.explanation: 'In the teeth of' means in spite of something like a problem or opposition. (किसी चीज़ के विरोध के बावजूद या सामने होने पर।)

questions.question: To "Kill two birds with one stone" means:
options: To achieve two results with a single effort
To try to do the impossible
To lose two opportunities at once
To be a very cruel hunter
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai ek teer se do nishane lagana, yani ek hi koshish mein do kaam nikal lena.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Keep someone in the loop":
options: To exclude someone from a group
To confuse someone with circular logic
To tie someone with a rope
To give someone all the necessary information and updates
questions.explanation: To "keep someone in the loop" means to keep them informed about a project or plan. (सारी जानकारी देते रहना या समूह में शामिल रखना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "In vogue":
options: Popular or in fashion
In a very difficult situation
In total darkness
Something that is outdated
questions.explanation: "In vogue" means something that is currently popular or trendy. (चलन में या लोकप्रिय होना।)

questions.question: "Know like the back of one's hand" implies:
options: To be very good at palmistry
To forget everything about a place
To have detailed knowledge of something
Having a very clean hand
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai kisi cheez ki bahut achhi aur vistrit jankari hona (raga-raga se waqif hona).

questions.question: To be in the "Lion's mouth" means:
options: To be the leader of a group
To be extremely hungry
A dangerous situation
To be a very famous circus performer
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai kisi khatarnak ya jokhim bhari sthiti mein hona.

questions.question: To "Lead someone by the nose" means:
options: To have a very sharp sense of smell
To follow someone's instructions blindly
To completely control or dominate someone
To help someone find their way in the dark
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi ko poori tarah apne kabu mein rakhna ya us par haavi hona.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "It's not rocket science":
options: Used to say that something is not very difficult to do or understand
A task that requires advanced scientific knowledge
A very expensive and complex project
To fail in a basic technical task
questions.explanation: This idiom is used to suggest that a task is simple and doesn't require great intelligence. (यह कोई बहुत कठिन काम नहीं है।)

questions.question: What does the idiom "In your birthday suit" mean?
options: Wearing your best expensive clothes
Wearing clothes that are too tight
Dressed specifically for a celebration
Not wearing any clothes
questions.explanation: "In your birthday suit" is a humorous way to say someone is completely naked. (पूरी तरह से नग्न होना।)

questions.question: What is the meaning of "If it's not one thing, it's another"?
options: To solve problems one by one
To have many choices in life
Everything is going perfectly
When one thing goes wrong, then another, and another
questions.explanation: This phrase is used when problems seem to happen one after another. (एक के बाद एक मुसीबतों का आना।)

questions.question: If you have "Latched on to something", you have:
options: Lost interest in it completely
Become firmly attached to it
Thrown it away in anger
Locked it inside a cupboard
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi cheez ya vichar se puri tarah jud jana ya use pakad lena.

questions.question: A decision made "Late in the day" is:
options: Made very early in the morning
Made at the perfect time
Too delayed to be of any use
Related to a sunset party
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai itni deri se koi kaam karna ki ab uska koi fayda na reh jaye.

questions.question: What is the meaning of "Hold one's tongue"?
options: To be silent / to keep quiet
To taste something bitter
To speak your mind boldly
To have a speech impediment
questions.explanation: Hold one's tongue means to refrain from speaking. (चुप रहना या जुबान पर लगाम रखना।)

questions.question: The phrase "Like oil and water" refers to:
options: A very expensive chemical reaction
Two things that mix perfectly well
Essential items for cooking a meal
Two people who are different from each other and cannot mix
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai do aise vyakti ya cheezein jo ek dusre se bilkul alag hon aur kabhi mil na sakein.

questions.question: What does the idiom "Keep under one's hat" mean?
options: To keep something a secret
To hide a small object under a hat
To wear a hat all the time
To be very protective of your clothes
questions.explanation: If you "keep something under your hat," you keep it secret and do not tell anyone. (किसी बात को गुप्त रखना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In lieu of
options: In place of or instead of
In addition to something else
In spite of the current circumstances
Because of a legal requirement
questions.explanation: 'In lieu of' is used to mean 'instead of' or as a substitute for. (के स्थान पर या के बदले में।)

questions.question: To "Lie low" means:
options: Try not to be noticed
To be physically short in height
To admit defeat in a very humble manner
To sleep on the floor instead of a bed
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai nazar mein na aana ya chip kar rehna taaki log aap par dhyan na dein.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Keep the ball rolling":
options: To play a game of football
To throw a ball at someone
To stop a project suddenly
To maintain progress or keep an activity going
questions.explanation: To "keep the ball rolling" means to make sure that an action or process continues. (प्रगति जारी रखना या काम चालू रखना।)

questions.question: What is the meaning of the idiom "It's raining cats and dogs"?
options: Animals falling from the sky
A sudden and unexpected storm
A light drizzle during the summer
Raining extremely heavily
questions.explanation: "Raining cats and dogs" means a very heavy downpour. (मूसलाधार बारिश होना।)

questions.question: "Like talking to a brick wall" means the listener is:
options: Hard of hearing
Very attentive and helpful
A construction worker
Inattentive or unresponsive
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi aise ko samjhana jo bilkul dhyan na de raha ho (bhains ke aage been bajana).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the fast lane
options: To be living a very dangerous and illegal life
To be driving above the speed limit
To be making progress faster than others
A life filled with great excitement and activity
questions.explanation: 'In the fast lane' refers to a lifestyle that is hectic, exciting, and highly active. (उत्साह और सक्रियता से भरपूर जीवन।)

questions.question: What does the idiom "Keep a straight face" mean?
options: To have a face with no wrinkles
Not laugh or change your expression even though you want to laugh
To look directly into someone's eyes
To be very honest and direct
questions.explanation: "Keep a straight face" means to remain serious even when something is very funny. (हंसी रोककर गंभीर बने रहना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In cahoots with
options: To compete fiercely against a rival
To be physically trapped in a small space
To be in an alliance or partnership with
To be in deep legal trouble with someone
questions.explanation: 'In cahoots with' means being in a secret partnership or alliance, often for a questionable purpose. (किसी के साथ मिलीभगत या साझेदारी में होना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Ivory towers":
options: A place where only wealthy people are allowed
Detachment and seclusion from the practical problems of everyday life
To be at the top of one's professional career
To live in a house made of expensive ivory
questions.explanation: "Ivory towers" refers to a state of privileged seclusion or separation from the facts and practicalities of the real world. (वास्तविक दुनिया की समस्याओं से दूर एक काल्पनिक या एकांत दुनिया में रहना।)

questions.question: What does the idiom "Jaundiced eye" mean?
options: To have very sharp eyesight
Looking at everything with great enthusiasm
A prejudiced or biased view
Suffering from a medical eye infection
questions.explanation: To look at something with a "jaundiced eye" means to be cynical or prejudiced because of past experiences. (पक्षपातपूर्ण दृष्टिकोण।)

questions.question: What is the meaning of the idiom "Latin and Greek"?
options: Unable to understand
To be a citizen of a European country
A very scholarly and academic discussion
To be proficient in multiple classical languages
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kuch aisa jo samajh se bahar ho (unable to understand).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Jog someone’s memory":
options: To disturb someone while they are thinking
To forget a very important event
To cause someone to remember something
To go for a run with an old friend
questions.explanation: To "jog someone's memory" means to give them a hint or reminder that helps them remember something. (याद दिलाना।)

questions.question: To "Lose count of" something implies:
options: To be an expert in mathematics
Forget the accurate total
To count things very slowly
To have a very sharp memory
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai ginti bhool jana kyunki sankhya bahut zyada hai.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In Dutch
options: To be in a state of luxury and comfort
To share the expenses of a meal equally
To follow a very strict and formal protocol
To be in trouble or disrepute
questions.explanation: 'In Dutch' is an idiom meaning to be in trouble with someone. (मुसीबत या परेशानी में होना।)

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning: "Hold the key"
options: To lose an important opportunity
To solve a mathematical puzzle
To be locked inside a room
To have control of something
questions.explanation: To hold the key means to have the power or the essential information to control a situation. (किसी चीज़ पर नियंत्रण होना या मुख्य रहस्य होना।)

questions.question: The phrase "In a nutshell" means:
options: To explain something in a very long and boring way
To hide a secret inside a shell
Briefly and concisely // In a short, clear way
To be in a very small and cramped place
questions.explanation: In a nutshell means to summarize the main points in a few words. (संक्षेप में या कम शब्दों में बताना।)

questions.question: What does "In a pickle" signify?
options: To be in a very happy and safe place
To preserve something for a long time
Experiencing a difficult situation // in trouble
To be fond of eating spicy food
questions.explanation: In a pickle is used when someone finds themselves in a mess or trouble. (कठिन परिस्थिति या मुसीबत में होना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "It's all in your head":
options: Something that is only your imagination
To have a very high IQ
To be extremely stubborn
Suffering from a severe headache
questions.explanation: "It's all in your head" means that something is not real and only exists in your mind. (यह केवल आपकी कल्पना है।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Keep one's head":
options: To lose one's temper quickly
To hold your head high in pride
To be very intelligent and smart
To remain calm in a difficult situation
questions.explanation: To "keep one's head" means to stay calm and sensible when in a crisis. (शांत रहना और आपा न खोना।)

questions.question: What is the meaning of "Looking forward to"?
options: Expecting with pleasure
Remembering the past with regret
Looking at the road ahead while driving
Moving physically to the front
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi aane wali cheez ka utsah aur khushi ke saath intezar karna.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the nick of time
options: To be exactly on the scheduled time
To be working according to a strict timetable
Just in time or at the last possible moment
To be late for an important appointment
questions.explanation: 'In the nick of time' means happening just before it is too late. (ठीक समय पर या अंतिम क्षण में।)

questions.question: Meaning of "Horses for courses":
options: Racing is a risky business
Different people are suited for different jobs
All people should be treated equally
To change one's mind frequently
questions.explanation: Horses for courses means that specific people have specific skills for specific tasks. (अलग-अलग काम के लिए अलग-अलग योग्य व्यक्ति।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the phrase "It's a small world":
options: To live in a very small house or locality
The earth is smaller than other planets
Meeting the same people unexpectedly in different places
To travel across the globe in a short time
questions.explanation: "It's a small world" is expressed when you meet someone you know in an unexpected place. (यह कितनी छोटी दुनिया है - जब अचानक कहीं कोई जान-पहचान वाला मिल जाए।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "It goes without saying":
options: A statement that has no logic
Something that is implied to be obvious
To travel to a place without telling anyone
To remain silent in a difficult meeting
questions.explanation: "It goes without saying" is used when something is so clear that it does not need to be stated. (यह कहने की आवश्यकता नहीं है क्योंकि यह स्पष्ट है।)

questions.question: What does the idiom "Keep up with the Joneses" mean?
options: To follow the legal rules of the neighborhood
To keep a close watch on the activities of others
To travel frequently with one's neighbors
Strive to match one's neighbors in spending and social standing
questions.explanation: This idiom refers to the desire to own the same expensive things as your neighbors or friends to avoid looking less successful. (सामाजिक स्तर और खर्च में पड़ोसियों की बराबरी करने की कोशिश करना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In high/good spirits
options: To be under the influence of alcohol
Full of hope and enthusiasm; very joyful
To be haunted by past memories
To be acting in a very supernatural manner
questions.explanation: 'In high spirits' means to be very happy, cheerful, and full of energy. (बहुत खुश, उत्साहित और जोश में होना।)

questions.question: Meaning of "Hope against hope":
options: Nurture an impossible hope
To have no hope at all
To be very certain of success
To pray for others' failure
questions.explanation: Hope against hope means to continue to hope even when the situation looks hopeless. (असंभव होते हुए भी उम्मीद बनाए रखना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the good books
options: To be a very studious or scholarly person
To be reading a high-quality novel
In favour with someone
To be mentioned in a historical record
questions.explanation: 'In the good books' means to be liked or approved of by someone. (किसी की नज़रों में अच्छा या प्रिय होना।)

questions.question: To "Let off steam" means:
options: To be very quiet and reserved
To cook food in a pressure cooker
To start a heavy fire
To work or play off excess energy or emotion
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai apne andar ki bhadaas nikalna ya apni faltu urja ko kisi kaam ya khel mein nikal dena.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the blink of an eye
options: Within a very short period of time; instantly
To be suffering from a vision impairment
To act with great hesitation and doubt
To be watching something with extreme focus
questions.explanation: 'In the blink of an eye' means something that happens extremely quickly. (पलक झपकते ही या बहुत ही कम समय में।)

questions.question: "Horse sense" refers to:
options: The ability to predict the future
Basic common sense
Knowledge about racing
A very high level of intelligence
questions.explanation: Horse sense is practical wisdom or common sense. (साधारण व्यावहारिक समझ।)

questions.question: To "Lock horns" with someone means:
options: To wear a decorative headgear
To agree with someone's opinion
To help someone in a struggle
Fight with someone
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi se bhid jana, jhagda karna ya behas karna.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the doldrums
options: In low spirits and despair
To be traveling in a calm sea area
To be confused between two difficult choices
To be extremely busy with multiple tasks
questions.explanation: 'In the doldrums' refers to a state of stagnation, depression, or low spirits. (उदास या उत्साहहीन होना।)

questions.question: If a person "Kept a level head" during a major crisis, it means they:
options: Was sensible and calm
Felt very dizzy and tired
Had a physical injury on their head
Were very confused and panicked
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai samajhdaari se kaam lena aur kathin samay mein shaant rehna.

questions.question: "I can't think straight" means:
options: To have a headache
To forget one's own name
Cannot think rationally due to being overwhelmed with emotion
To be very honest in thinking
questions.explanation: This idiom describes a state of confusion or emotional distress. (भावनाओं के कारण सही से न सोच पाना।)

questions.question: Two people who are "Like two peas in a pod" are:
options: Always fighting with each other
Very different in their opinions
Farmers who grow vegetables
Look very similar in appearance or character
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai do logon ka dikhne mein ya swabhav mein bilkul ek jaisa hona.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the idiom "Judas kiss"?
options: A deceitful pretence of friendship
An act of great sacrifice for a friend
To greet a stranger with a smile
A kiss shared between true lovers
questions.explanation: A "Judas kiss" refers to an act of betrayal disguised as an act of affection. (मैत्री का झूठा दिखावा या धोखा।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In cold blood
options: To be shivering due to extreme fear
To do something cruelly and without emotion
To act out of sudden, uncontrollable anger
To have a naturally low body temperature
questions.explanation: 'In cold blood' means to commit an act (usually a crime) deliberately and without any pity or feeling. (निर्दयतापूर्वक और बिना किसी भावना के।)

questions.question: What does "Laying off" refer to in a business sense?
options: Taking a long vacation from work
Giving a promotion to hardworking staff
Hiring new employees for a project
Dismissal from jobs
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai karmchariyon ko naukri se nikal dena (Dismissal from jobs).

questions.question: "Look before you leap" means:
options: To jump as high as possible
Think carefully of the consequences
To be very brave and take risks
To run away from a dangerous animal
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai koi bhi kaam karne ya kadam uthane se pehle uske natijon ke baare mein soch lena.

questions.question: If someone says "Lend me your ear", they are asking you:
options: To give them a physical organ
To pay attention
To keep a secret
To ignore what they are saying
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi se dhyan se sunne ki guzarish karna (politely ask for attention).

questions.question: What does the idiom "Keep one's word" mean?
options: To repeat a word many times
To refuse to speak to anyone
To have a large vocabulary
To uphold one's promise
questions.explanation: "Keep one's word" means to do what you promised you would do. (वादा निभाना।)

questions.question: What is meant by "Loaves and fishes"?
options: To share food with the poor
A balanced seafood diet
Materialist wealth or personal profit
To be a very religious person
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai bhautik sukh-suvidhaein ya swarthi labh.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In full swing
options: To be indecisive and changing opinions frequently
To be playing a game with maximum intensity
To be physically moving on a swing set
To be at the height of activity or very active
questions.explanation: 'In full swing' means a situation that has reached its highest level of activity. (पूरी सक्रियता या पूरे जोरों पर होना।)

questions.question: What is the meaning of the phrase "Keep someone posted"?
options: To ignore someone's messages
To send a physical letter via post
To keep sharing the latest information with someone
To appoint someone to a high post
questions.explanation: "Keep someone posted" means to regularly give someone the most recent news about a situation. (ताज़ा जानकारी देते रहना।)

questions.question: Being "Like a cat on a hot tin roof" refers to:
options: Being in a state of agitation or restlessness
Being very lucky in difficult times
Being very agile and fast
Enjoying the warmth of the sun in winter
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai bahut bechain ya vyakul hona (jaise garam chhat par baithi billi).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Keep abreast of":
options: To keep oneself updated with the latest information
To compete with someone in a race
To stay physically close to someone
To hide information from others
questions.explanation: "Keep abreast of" means to stay up to date with the latest developments or news. (ताज़ा जानकारी से अवगत रहना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the egg
options: To be preparing for a grand celebration
To be in a very fragile condition
To be in an early stage
To be hidden from the outside world
questions.explanation: 'In the egg' means something that is in its very beginning or early stage. (शुरुआती अवस्था में होना।)

questions.question: The phrase "Lie in the bed one has made" implies:
options: To enjoy the comfort of a well-made bed
Face the unpleasant consequences of your own acts
To sleep peacefully after a long day of work
To avoid taking responsibility for one's mistakes
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai ki apne kiye gaye kaamon ke bure natije khud hi bhugatna (jaisa boyoge waisa katoge).

questions.question: What does "How time flies!" signify?
options: Time is moving very slowly
Time is standing still
Time passes quickly
One is wasting time
questions.explanation: This expression is used to remark on how fast time is going by. (समय का बहुत तेज़ी से बीत जाना।)

questions.question: The phrase "Horse around" means:
options: To behave in a silly and noisy way
To take care of animals
To work like a slave
To run very fast in a competition
questions.explanation: Horse around means to engage in rough or lighthearted play. (मौज-मस्ती या शोर-शराबे वाली बेवकूफी भरी हरकतें करना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the same breath
options: To be suffering from a respiratory illness
To be breathing in a synchronized manner
To be in total agreement with someone
Say two contradictory things at the same time
questions.explanation: 'In the same breath' is used when someone says two things that are very different or opposite nearly at once. (एक ही समय में दो विरोधाभासी बातें कहना।)

questions.question: What is the meaning of the phrase "Keep your shirt on"?
options: To wear formal clothes in an office
To remain cool and patient
To be ready for a physical fight
To refuse to share your belongings with others
questions.explanation: "Keep your shirt on" is an informal way to tell someone to calm down or be patient. (शांत और धैर्यवान बने रहना।)

questions.question: To be "Like a shag on a rock" means:
options: To be a professional fisherman
To be surrounded by many friends
Completely alone
To be very strong and steady
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai poori tarah se akela ya nirashrit hona.

questions.question: What does the idiom "Jumping down my throat" mean?
options: To offer someone food very quickly
To react with extreme praise
To scold or shout at someone angrily
To be confused by someone's words
questions.explanation: If someone is "jumping down your throat," they are scolding you very severely or reacting over-critically. (बुरी तरह डांटना।)

questions.question: The phrase "Hue and cry" means:
options: To cry in loneliness
A noisy expression of anger / protest / loud clamour
A colorful painting
To celebrate a victory silently
questions.explanation: Hue and cry refers to a loud public outcry or protest. (शोर-शराबा या ज़ोरदार विरोध।)

questions.question: "Last but not least" means:
options: The final person is the least important
The first person is the most important
Last in order of mention but not last in importance
To be the last one to finish a race
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai ki bhale hi zikr sabse aakhir mein ho raha hai, par mahatva (importance) kam nahi hai.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the idiom "Level playing field"?
options: A sports ground that is perfectly flat and grassy
A competition where the stronger side always has an advantage
A situation where everyone has a fair and equal chance of succeeding
A playground built for children with special needs
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai aisi sthiti jahan sabhi ko barabar ka mauka mile aur kisi ke paas koi anuchit fayda na ho.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the driver's seat
options: To be learning a new professional skill
To be traveling in a luxury vehicle
To be the only one responsible for a mistake
To be in charge or in control of a situation
questions.explanation: 'In the driver's seat' means to be in a position of authority or control. (नियंत्रण या जिम्मेदारी वाली स्थिति में होना।)

questions.question: To "Lose one's head" signifies:
options: To lose self control or panic
To forget one's own name
To be very brave in a battle
To have a headache
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai apna aapa kho dena, ghabra jana ya darr mein pagal ho jana.

questions.question: To "Knock down" a building means:
options: To build it from scratch
To paint it beautifully
To sell it at a high price
To demolish it
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi dhanchay ya imarat ko gira dena ya dhwast kar dena.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the soup
options: To be in trouble or in a difficult situation
To be an expert in culinary arts
To be mixing different ideas together
To be enjoying a delicious meal
questions.explanation: 'In the soup' is an informal way of saying someone is in serious trouble. (मुसीबत या परेशानी में होना।)

questions.question: To "Lend someone a hand" means:
options: To help or assist, especially voluntarily
To take something from someone
To point at someone's mistake
To shake hands as a greeting
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai kisi ki madad karna ya sahayata dena.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the heat of the moment
options: To be working in a very high-temperature environment
To be at the peak of one's professional career
Saying or doing something without thinking due to anger or excitement
To suffer from a heatstroke during summer
questions.explanation: 'In the heat of the moment' refers to actions taken while overwhelmed by temporary emotions. (बिना सोचे-समझे आवेश में आकर कुछ करना।)

questions.question: To "Look into" a matter means:
options: To consider someone inferior
To be careful of surroundings
To ignore the problem completely
To investigate
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi mamle ki jaanch-partal karna.

questions.question: If a team "Lost by a neck", the margin of defeat was:
options: Very large and humiliating
They didn't lose at all
A small margin
Exactly one meter
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai bahut hi kam antar ya maamuli margin se haar jana.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the swim
options: To be struggling to stay afloat financially
To be flowing with the general public opinion
To be physically swimming in a pool
Well-informed and up-to-date with current affairs
questions.explanation: 'In the swim' means to be active in or knowing about what is going on in a particular area. (पूरी तरह सूचित या जानकारी में होना।)

questions.question: To "Learn by heart" means:
options: To feel very emotional about a subject
To memorize something
To learn something very slowly
To forget everything you studied
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi cheez ko poori tarah yaad kar lena (ratta marna ya kanthasth karna).

questions.question: The idiom "Hold good" means:
options: To behave well in public
To remain valid
To keep a secret well
To be physically strong
questions.explanation: Hold good means to continue to be true or applicable. (मान्य रहना या लागू रहना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In harmony with
options: To be talented in musical composition
To be following a rhythmic schedule
To be in complete agreement or accord with
To live in a physically quiet environment
questions.explanation: 'In harmony with' means to be in agreement or to be consistent with something/someone. (तालमेल या सहमति में होना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Jump the gun":
options: To win a shooting competition
To run away from a dangerous situation
To criticize someone without proof
To start something too soon or act hastily
questions.explanation: "Jump the gun" means to do something before the right time. (समय से पहले या जल्दबाजी में कोई काम शुरू करना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the ascendant
options: To be looking at the stars for guidance
Rising in power, influence, or importance
To be physically climbing a steep mountain
To be descending from a position of authority
questions.explanation: 'In the ascendant' means gaining more power, influence, or popularity. (शक्ति या प्रभाव में बढ़ते हुए होना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In harness
options: To be in office or performing one's routine work
To be preparing for a long journey
To be physically tied to a heavy load
To be under strict punishment or arrest
questions.explanation: 'In harness' means to be engaged in one's regular work or occupation. (अपने काम या सेवा में लगे होना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In raptures
options: To be extremely delighted or enthusiastic
To be caught in a sudden natural disaster
To be in a state of violent anger
To be suffering from a physical injury
questions.explanation: 'In raptures' means to be in a state of great joy or intense pleasure. (अत्यधिक प्रसन्न या मंत्रमुग्ध होना।)

questions.question: The term "Laurels" in a professional context refers to:
options: To earn great prestige or distinction
To retire early from service
A type of decorative plant in the office
To be punished for a mistake
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai bahut maan-samman ya pratishtha prapt karna.

questions.question: To "Look after" someone means:
options: Take care of
To look at someone after they leave
To follow someone secretly
To search for a lost person
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai kisi ki dekhbhal karna ya khayal rakhna.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the phrase "In a jiffy"?
options: To do something with great difficulty
Something that is done very quickly // Suddenly
To act after a lot of hesitation
Something that takes a long time to complete
questions.explanation: In a jiffy refers to something happening almost instantly. (बहुत जल्दी या अचानक कुछ होना।)

questions.question: A "Large-hearted" person is:
options: Generous and kind
Someone with a serious medical condition
Someone who is very tall and heavy
Very greedy and selfish
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai udaar ya bade dil wala vyakti jo dusron ki madad kare.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In line with
options: To be next in the order of succession
Standing in a physical queue
Following a straight geographical path
In agreement or conformity with
questions.explanation: 'In line with' means to be consistent with or in agreement with a rule, principle, or someone's view. (की कतार में होना या किसी चीज़ के अनुरूप/सहमति में होना।)

questions.question: If someone is "Laying it on thick", they are:
options: Being very honest and direct
Painting a wall with multiple layers
Telling a very boring and long story
An exaggeration
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi baat ko bahut badha-chadhakar kehna (exaggeration).

questions.question: If a student is told to "Hit the books", what are they expected to do?
options: To write a new novel
To clean the library shelves
To throw away old textbooks
To study very hard
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai bahut mehnat se padhai karna (To study very hard).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the blues
options: To be navigating deep ocean waters
Cheerless, depressed, or low-spirited
To be a member of a royal or noble family
To be wearing a formal blue uniform
questions.explanation: 'In the blues' means to feel sad, depressed, or discouraged. (दुखी, उदास या निराशा की स्थिति में होना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the same boat
options: To be working for the same shipping company
In the same unpleasant or difficult situation
To be traveling together on a cruise
To have exactly the same hobbies as others
questions.explanation: 'In the same boat' means to be in the same difficult circumstances as others. (एक ही जैसी कठिन परिस्थिति में होना।)

questions.question: When soldiers "Laid down their arms", they:
options: Surrendered
Picked up new weapons
Started a new physical exercise
Won the battle decisively
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai hathiyar daal dena ya aatmasamarpan kar dena.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the phrase "Iron fist"?
options: To be very lazy in performing tasks
To handle something very strictly
To be physically very strong
To have a hand made of metal
questions.explanation: To rule or manage with an "iron fist" means to exercise power in a very strict and harsh way. (कठोरता से शासन या नियंत्रण करना।)

questions.question: What does "Light at the end of the tunnel" signify?
options: The final exit of a long railway journey
A dangerous fire inside a dark cave
A torch carried by a traveler in the dark
Seeing signs of improvement in the future
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai kathin paristhiti mein ummeed ki kiran dikhna ya sthiti mein sudhaar ke sanket milna.

questions.question: To "Lay hands on" someone usually implies:
options: To touch or grasp someone, often with the threat of violence
To give them a warm blessing
To help someone stand up after a fall
To praise someone for their hard work
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi ko pakadna ya chhuna, aksar hinsa ya darr dikhane ke irade se.

questions.question: What does it mean to be "Like a phoenix"?
options: To be a legendary creature with magical powers
To be very beautiful and colorful
To die a very sudden and tragic death
With a new life or rising from the ashes
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai poori tarah se barbaad hone ke baad phir se naye jeevan ya urja ke saath khada hona.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Keep up appearances":
options: To pretend to be happier or richer so as to conceal the real situation
To go to a beauty parlor to look better
To always wear expensive branded clothes
To maintain a house by cleaning it regularly
questions.explanation: "Keep up appearances" means maintaining an impression of wealth or well-being despite financial or personal difficulties. (वास्तविक स्थिति को छिपाने के लिए खुश या अमीर होने का दिखावा करना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Keep in check":
options: To place something in a box
To keep a bank cheque safely
To verify the truth of a statement
To control or restrain something
questions.explanation: To "keep in check" means to keep something under control so it doesn't become too great or powerful. (नियंत्रण में रखना।)

questions.question: Meaning of "Husband our resources":
options: Save / use economically
To marry for wealth
To give away all belongings
To spend money lavishly
questions.explanation: To husband resources means to manage or conserve them carefully. (संसाधनों की बचत करना या उन्हें संजो कर रखना।)

questions.question: What is the meaning of the phrase "Kept a stiff upper lip"?
options: To have a dental problem
Remained expressionless or showed fortitude
To be extremely angry and shouting
To be very talkative
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai mushkil ghadi mein apne jazbaat na dikhana aur bura waqt bina shikayat ke jhelna.

questions.question: "Icing on the cake" refers to:
options: To hide the flaws of something
An unnecessary addition to a problem
A very sweet dessert
Something that makes a good situation even better
questions.explanation: Icing on the cake is an extra benefit that makes a good thing even better. (सोने पर सुहागा / किसी अच्छी स्थिति को और बेहतर बनाना।)

questions.question: "Leaves you in the lurch" means:
options: To go on a holiday with a friend
To provide help in a difficult time
To give someone a lot of money
To leave someone in a helpless condition
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai kisi ko mushkil ghadi mein akela ya asahay chhod dena.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In dire straits
options: To be navigating through narrow sea passages
In a very bad or difficult situation
To be at a critical crossroads in life
To be experiencing a temporary financial lapse
questions.explanation: 'In dire straits' describes being in a very miserable, difficult, or dangerous situation. (बहुत बुरी या कठिन स्थिति में होना।)

questions.question: What is the meaning of the idiom "Keep an ear to the ground"?
options: To stay informed and updated about everything
To listen to the sounds of nature
To be very tired and lie down
To ignore all rumors and gossips
questions.explanation: To "keep an ear to the ground" means to pay attention to what is happening or what people are saying. (होने वाली गतिविधियों या अफवाहों पर नज़र रखना।)

questions.question: What does the idiom "Keep in touch" mean?
options: To be very sensitive to others' feelings
To physically touch someone frequently
To lose contact after moving away
To maintain contact with another person
questions.explanation: "Keep in touch" means to continue to communicate with someone. (संपर्क बनाए रखना।)

questions.question: Who are referred to as "Kith and kin"?
options: People from different countries
Distant neighbors and colleagues
Blood relatives
Enemies and rivals
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai apne sage-sambandhi ya rishtedaar.

questions.question: The idiom "In a flutter" refers to:
options: In a nervous state
To be completely motionless
To fly like a bird
To be in a very happy mood
questions.explanation: In a flutter means to be excited, confused, or nervous. (घबराहट या उत्तेजना की स्थिति में होना।)

questions.question: To be "Left out in the cold" means:
options: To be ignored
To enjoy a snowy winter morning
To be kicked out of a house
To suffer from a common cold
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai kisi ko nazarandaz karna ya kisi samuh (group) se alag-thalag kar dena.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the running
options: To be physically exercising daily
To be managing a very large organization
Contesting a seat or having a chance of success
To be fleeing from a legal responsibility
questions.explanation: 'In the running' means to have a reasonable chance of winning or being chosen. (प्रतियोगिता या सफलता की दौड़ में होना।)

questions.question: If you "Know the ropes", you:
options: Are very good at tying knots
Know how to do a job or task properly
Are confused about the basic details
Are a professional mountain climber
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai kisi kaam ki barikiyaan ya use karne ka sahi tarika janna.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the limelight
options: To be working as a professional light technician
To be eating very sour or acidic food
The centre of attraction or public attention
To be living a very quiet and isolated life
questions.explanation: 'In the limelight' means to be the focus of public attention or interest. (चर्चा या आकर्षण का केंद्र होना।)

questions.question: If a group of people "Kicked up a row", they:
options: Arranged the seats in a straight line
Made a great fuss or caused a disturbance
Won a rowing competition comfortably
Left the room very quietly
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai zor-daar hungama khada karna ya shor-sharaba karna.

questions.question: Select the meaning of "In a tight corner/spot":
options: In a difficult situation
To hide from everyone in a corner
To live in a very large mansion
To be in a safe and comfortable position
questions.explanation: In a tight corner means being in a situation that is hard to deal with. (कठिन परिस्थिति में होना।)

questions.question: The idiom "In apple pie order" refers to:
options: To be in a state of total chaos
To cook a delicious dessert
To follow a very strict diet
In perfect order // Arranged neatly
questions.explanation: In apple pie order means everything is perfectly organized. (बिल्कुल व्यवस्थित या साफ-सुथरा होना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Keep a low profile":
options: To be physically short in height
To work in a position of low authority
To avoid attracting attention to yourself
To live in a basement or a ground floor
questions.explanation: To "keep a low profile" means to behave in a way that doesn't attract public notice. (अपने आप को चर्चा से दूर रखना या ध्यान आकर्षित न करना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the dark
options: To be in ignorance or not know something others are aware of
To work secretly during the night time
To be afraid of the unknown or future
To be in a place with no electricity
questions.explanation: 'In the dark' means to be unaware or uninformed about a particular situation. (किसी बात से अनभिज्ञ होना या जानकारी न होना।)

questions.question: Meaning of "I have my hands full":
options: To carry many bags while shopping
To be very busy with many responsibilities
To be a very greedy person
To be very rich
questions.explanation: Having hands full means being completely occupied with work. (बहुत व्यस्त होना या बहुत सारी ज़िम्मेदारियाँ होना।)

questions.question: What does it mean to "Lay out" a large sum of money?
options: To spend
To earn money through hard work
To hide money in a secret place
To save money for the future
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hota hai paise kharch karna (Spend).

questions.question: "Kicking his heels" means:
options: Buying new expensive shoes
Wasting time while waiting for something
Running very fast to catch a bus
Dancing with great joy
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai bekaar baithe rehna ya intezar karte hue samay barbad karna.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In deep water
options: To be an expert in a particular complex field
To be in a state of great difficulty or trouble
To have a wealth of hidden resources
To be physically submerged in a water body
questions.explanation: 'In deep water' refers to being in a very difficult or serious situation. (गंभीर मुसीबत या कठिनाई में होना।)

questions.question: What does it mean to "Let your hair down"?
options: To feel sad and disappointed
To lose one's temper in public
Behave freely and uninhibitedly // To take it easy and relax
To go to a professional hair stylist
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai poori tarah se relax karna aur bina kisi sankoch ya bandhan ke maze karna.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the eye of a storm
options: To be witnessing a conflict from a distance
To be the cause of a major disaster
To be safe while others are in trouble
To be in the middle of a difficult or controversial situation
questions.explanation: 'In the eye of a storm' means to be deeply involved in a difficult or contentious situation. (मुसीबत या विवाद के केंद्र में होना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the pink
options: To be extremely embarrassed or shy
In good health or peak physical condition
To be a very young and inexperienced person
To be surrounded by luxury and wealth
questions.explanation: 'In the pink' means to be in very good health or spirits. (बेहतर स्वास्थ्य या अच्छी स्थिति में होना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Keeps an open house":
options: To leave the doors unlocked at night
A house that has no roof or windows
Welcomes all members or visitors
To sell one's property to the highest bidder
questions.explanation: A person who "keeps an open house" is always ready to provide hospitality to any guest or visitor. (सभी मेहमानों या सदस्यों का स्वागत करना।)

questions.question: What does the phrase "Little by little" signify?
options: To be very stingy with money
Suddenly and unexpectedly
In a very small amount that is useless
Gradually
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai dheere-dheere ya kramashah.

questions.question: The phrase "Hold out" means:
options: To endure a difficult situation
To support a friend in need
To be very happy
To reach for something far away
questions.explanation: Hold out means to continue to survive or resist in a tough situation. (कठिन परिस्थिति में टिके रहना या झेलना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Keep at arm’s length":
options: To measure the length of one's arm
To help someone with all your strength
To hug someone very tightly
To be at a distance with someone or avoid being friendly
questions.explanation: To "keep someone at arm's length" means to avoid becoming too friendly or close to them. (दूरी बनाए रखना या दोस्ती न बढ़ाना।)

questions.question: What is the meaning of the idiom "Hit a dead end"?
options: To solve a complex mystery easily
To reach a situation that leads to nothing further
To start a new successful venture
To drive a vehicle into a wall
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai aisi sthiti par pahunch jana jisse aage nahi badha ja sakta (To reach a situation that leads to nothing further).

questions.question: What does it mean to "Lose face"?
options: Become embarrassed or less respected by others
To look younger than one's age
To have a physical injury on the face
To wear a mask to a party
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai apmanit hona ya apni pratishtha (respect) kho dena.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: "Hit the jackpot"
options: To lose all savings in a gamble
To make money quickly or find exactly what was sought
To work extremely hard for a living
To hit someone accidentally
questions.explanation: Hit the jackpot means to find unexpected success or wealth. (अचानक बहुत सारा धन मिलना या मनचाही चीज़ मिल जाना।)

questions.question: "Hold your horses" is used to tell someone to:
options: Be patient / slow down / restrain oneself
Start a race immediately
Ride a horse faster
Buy a new stable
questions.explanation: Hold your horses is an informal way to tell someone to wait or be patient. (धैर्य रखना या जल्दबाज़ी न करना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: "Ignorance is bliss"
options: To gain knowledge through difficult experiences
To remain ignorant of things that may cause stress
To be highly educated and successful
To be aware of every detail of a problem
questions.explanation: Ignorance is bliss means it is better not to know about bad things that you cannot change. (ऐसी चीज़ों से अनजान रहना जो तनाव का कारण बन सकती हैं।)

questions.question: If someone is "Looking for greener pastures", they are:
options: Exploring new opportunities
Buying a farm in the countryside
Painting their house green
Looking for a missing animal
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai behtar maukon ya nayi sambhavnaon ki talash karna (exploring new opportunities).

questions.question: "Let sleeping dogs lie" means:
options: To keep a guard dog at home
To be very kind to animals
Do not bring up an old controversial issue
To wake up someone who is lazy
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai purane vivadon ya jhagdon ko na ukhadna taaki koi nayi mushkil khadi na ho.

questions.question: "Lock, stock and barrel" means:
options: To be very careful with one's belongings
Including all or every part of something (Completely)
Parts of a very old gun
To lock everything in a warehouse
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai poori tarah se ya sab kuch samet kar.

questions.question: What does the idiom "Keep count of" mean?
options: To remember a total or track something
To count numbers out loud
To forget how many items were present
To participate in a math competition
questions.explanation: "Keep count of" means to track the number of something as it increases or changes. (गिनती रखना या याद रखना।)

questions.question: Something happening "Left, right and centre" means:
options: Happening in a very organized manner
Happening only in the middle of the city
Happening in a lot of places or to a lot of people
Not happening at all
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai har jagah ya bahut se logon ke saath ek saath hona.

questions.question: The idiom "Hold water" means:
options: To be a good swimmer
To be full of emotions
To store water for the future
To seem acceptable / appear to be valid or reasonable
questions.explanation: If an argument "holds water," it is logical and sound. (तर्कसंगत होना या मान्य प्रतीत होना।)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In the loop
options: Informed regularly about a situation
To be part of a high-level conspiracy
To be physically tied up in a rope
To be trapped in a repeating sequence of events
questions.explanation: 'In the loop' means to be part of a group that is kept informed about something. (पूरी जानकारी होना या नियमित रूप से सूचित होना।)

questions.question: "Let bygones be bygones" implies:
options: To forget past offences and be reconciled
To repeat the same conflict again
To remember past mistakes forever
To punish someone for old crimes
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai purani baaton ya jhagdon ko bhoolkar aage badhna (maaf kar dena).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom: In queer street
options: To be exploring an unknown territory
To be in debt or financial difficulty
To be mentally confused or eccentric
To live in a very colorful and vibrant neighborhood
questions.explanation: 'In queer street' is an old idiom meaning to be in a difficult situation, especially regarding money or debt. (कर्ज या आर्थिक कठिनाई में होना।)

questions.question: "Loaded words" refers to:
options: Words used by wealthy people
Words that are very difficult to pronounce
Words that carry more significance than their literal meaning
A long list of vocabulary in a book
questions.explanation: Iska arth hai aise shabd jinka gehra arth ho ya jo bhavnaon se bhare hon.

questions.question: To "Lap up" information means to:
options: Reject it immediately
Forget it very soon
Accept it eagerly
Criticize it harshly
questions.explanation: Iska matlab hai kisi cheez (khabar ya tareef) ko bahut utsah ke saath sweekar karna.

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the phrase: "Hit the nail on the head"
options: To cause a physical injury to someone
To be confused about a simple task
To make a repetitive mistake
To find exactly the right answer or do something exactly right
questions.explanation: This idiom means to say or do something that is precisely correct. (बिल्कुल सही बात कहना या सही सटीक काम करना।)
